audit_status.source_base: Greek 2 Thessalonians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for day-of-Lord correction, rebellion/apostasy, lawless one, restrainer, mystery of lawlessness, delusion language, election/standing firm. Non-sensational handling required.

verses.restored: Now we ask you, siblings, concerning the presence/arrival of our Lord Jesus Messiah and our gathering together to him,
that you not be quickly shaken from mind nor troubled, neither through spirit nor through word nor through letter as if from us, as though the day of the Lord has arrived.
Let no one deceive you in any way; because unless the rebellion/departure comes first and the human of lawlessness is unveiled, the son of ruin,
the one opposing and lifting himself above every so-called god or object of reverence, so that he sits in the temple of the Divine, displaying himself as if he is divine.
Do you not remember that while I was still with you, I was saying these things to you?
And now you know what restrains, so that he may be unveiled in his own season.
For the mystery of lawlessness is already working; only the one restraining now will do so until out of the midst.
And then the lawless one will be unveiled, whom the Lord Jesus will consume by the breath of his mouth and make inactive by the appearance of his presence/arrival,
whose presence/arrival is according to the working of the adversarial one in all power and signs and wonders of falsehood,
and in every deceit of injustice among those perishing, because they did not receive the love of the truth so that they might be saved/healed.
And because of this the Divine sends them a working of delusion, so that they trust the falsehood,
so that all may be judged who did not trust the truth but took pleasure in injustice.

But we ought always to give thanks to the Divine concerning you, siblings beloved by the Lord, because the Divine chose you as firstfruit for salvation/healing in sanctification of Spirit and trust of truth,
into which he called you through our good news, into obtaining glory of our Lord Jesus Messiah.
So then, siblings, stand firm and hold fast the traditions you were taught, whether through word or through our letter.
Now may our Lord Jesus Messiah himself and Divine our Father, the One having loved us and given age-long comfort/encouragement and good hope in grace,
comfort/encourage your hearts and establish them in every good work and word.

translation_notes: This chapter must be handled non-sensationally. The lawless one and restraining force are not identified in the text; preserve ambiguity rather than mapping them onto modern targets.
Mystery of lawlessness names a hidden pattern already at work, not a license for paranoia or accusation.
The pastoral command is not panic but steadiness: do not be shaken, stand firm, hold fast, be comforted and established.
